message:
fix(interpreter): fail closed on instanceof/in operators in baseline heap

The baseline interpreter's simplified heap is not prototype-aware, so
instanceof and in operators were returning incorrect results by checking
only the direct constructor function or own properties, ignoring the
prototype chain entirely. This could silently produce wrong answers for
any code relying on prototype inheritance.

Replace the incorrect implementations with fail-closed semantics:
- instanceof: validate operand types (rhs must be function, lhs object
  must exist in heap) then return UnsupportedMembershipSemantics error
- in: validate target is an object that exists in heap, validate key
  register is readable, then return UnsupportedMembershipSemantics error

Add InterpreterError::UnsupportedMembershipSemantics variant with
descriptive message noting that object_model.rs remains the semantic
source of truth until the baseline interpreter can faithfully reuse
that substrate.

Update module documentation to explain the fail-closed rationale.
Update integration tests for the new error behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
